title: NOC 11202 – Professional Occupations in Advertising, Marketing and Public Relations | GTR Immigration
description: Professionals working under NOC 11202 are essential to brand development, public communication, digital growth, and fundraising strategies across Canada. With rapid expansion in digital marketing, communications, and corporate outreach, this occupation offers strong prospects for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) applicants.

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.minor_group: 1120 – Human resources and business service professionals

duties: Professionals in this group may perform some or all of the following:
Evaluate products or services to define advertising and marketing strategies
Advise organizations on promotional opportunities and brand positioning
Design and implement digital and direct marketing campaigns
Manage social media channels and online reputation
Develop and execute public relations strategies
Conduct opinion surveys and audience research
Prepare reports, speeches, websites, and press releases
Organize publicity events, workshops, and fundraising campaigns
Act as spokespersons and manage media relations
Represent creative professionals and negotiate contracts
employment_requirements: University degree or college diploma in marketing, communications, journalism, public relations, or related disciplines
APR (Accredited in Public Relations) designation for some PR roles
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is expected to create 73,700 new jobs for advertising, marketing, and public relations professionals, while approximately 67,200 job seekers may enter the field. For candidates from India and other countries, this represents strong immigration potential with effective planning and credential recognition.
labour_market_demand: Advertising, marketing, and public relations professionals remain in strong demand nationwide as organizations invest in digital branding, public engagement, and market research.
High hiring levels across agencies, corporations, and government bodies
Excellent immigration prospects for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $34,700 to $101,800, depending on experience, province, and specialization.
Why Advertising & Marketing Professionals Choose Canada
Secure employment in dynamic creative industries
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
